name: Santa Ana la Real - Castaño del Robledo - Alájar – circular through the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park
name_original: Santa Ana la Real - Castaño del Robledo - Alájar – circular por el Parque Natural de Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Ana La Real offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park. The region is characterized by Mediterranean forests, cork oak and holm oak groves, and river valleys. Notable natural features include the Los Chorros Waterfalls, the impressive Risco Levante stone tower, and Cerro Castillejo, a hill offering panoramic views. The varied terrain provides a scenic backdrop for all levels of hiking. Best hiking trails around Santa Ana La Real The most popular hiking route is Risco Levante-Los Chorros Trail, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route connects the Risco Levante with the Los Chorros waterfalls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Joyarancón Waterfall – Santa Ana to Alájar Trail loop from Santa Ana la Real, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail features the Joyarancón Waterfall and explores the natural park's varied scenery. Local hikers also love the Santa Ana la Real – Joyarancón Waterfall loop from Santa Ana la Real, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 11 minutes. Hiking around Santa Ana La Real is defined by Mediterranean forests, river valleys, and waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging routes. The routes in Santa Ana La Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Ana La Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking around Santa Ana La Real?
A: The region is rich in diverse natural features. You'll encounter the iconic &lt;strong&gt;Los Chorros Waterfalls&lt;/strong&gt;, especially impressive during the rainy season, and the striking &lt;strong&gt;Risco Levante&lt;/strong&gt;, a natural stone tower. Many trails also follow the lush &lt;em&gt;Rivera de Santa Ana&lt;/em&gt;. For panoramic views, consider routes that ascend &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4086077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Cristóbal&lt;/a&gt; or the emblematic Cerro Castillejo.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls in Santa Ana La Real?
A: Yes, waterfalls are a prominent feature in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983585106/risco-levante-los-chorros-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Risco Levante-Los Chorros Trail&lt;/a&gt; directly leads to the impressive Los Chorros Waterfalls. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14620946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joyarancón Waterfall – Santa Ana to Alájar Trail loop from Santa Ana la Real&lt;/a&gt;, which features the Joyarancón Waterfall.

Q: What are some easy hiking options for beginners or families in Santa Ana La Real?
A: Santa Ana La Real offers several easy trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17265770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana to Alájar Trail – Dehesa del Ibérico loop from Santa Ana la Real&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.1 km path. The &lt;em&gt;Sendero Bosque de las Letras&lt;/em&gt; (Forest of Letters Trail) is also designed to be accessible, integrating nature with cultural elements and features for people with reduced mobility.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Santa Ana La Real?
A: Yes, many trails in the area are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points in the village. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14620946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joyarancón Waterfall – Santa Ana to Alájar Trail loop from Santa Ana la Real&lt;/a&gt; (8.5 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14620962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana la Real – Joyarancón Waterfall loop from Santa Ana la Real&lt;/a&gt; (11 km). For a longer challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984185938/santa-ana-la-real-castano-del-robledo-alajar-circular-through-the-sierra-de-aracena-y-picos-de-aroche-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana la Real - Castaño del Robledo - Alájar circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.6 km.

Q: What cultural or historical points of interest can I find on the trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, the trails offer cultural insights. The &lt;em&gt;Sendero Bosque de las Letras&lt;/em&gt; (Forest of Letters Trail) features literary fragments and poems along its path. You can also explore the historical lime kilns on the &lt;em&gt;Sendero Hornos de Cal (SL-A 201)&lt;/em&gt;, showcasing the region's heritage.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Santa Ana La Real?
A: The hiking routes in Santa Ana La Real are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the stunning natural features like the waterfalls and panoramic viewpoints.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Ana La Real?
A: The Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly popular due to milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. During the rainy season, the waterfalls are at their most spectacular. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or panoramic spots accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several stunning viewpoints. The emblematic Cerro Castillejo, located behind Santa Ana La Real, provides spectacular panoramic views of the village and surrounding countryside. You can also find great vistas from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2894628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alájar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4086077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Cristóbal&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible via various routes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Santa Ana La Real?
A: Many trails within the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Please ensure you respect local regulations and clean up after your pet to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: How many hiking trails are available around Santa Ana La Real?
A: There are over 18 hiking routes available around Santa Ana La Real, catering to various skill levels. This includes 6 easy trails, 10 moderate trails, and 2 more challenging routes, ensuring options for every hiker.

Q: Are there any unique experiences like night hiking available?
A: Yes, the region offers unique opportunities such as &lt;em&gt;Night Hiking&lt;/em&gt; (Sendero Nocturno) and an &lt;em&gt;Astronomical Route&lt;/em&gt; (Ruta Astronómica). These provide a different perspective on the natural environment, allowing you to explore under the stars.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the trails?
A: The terrain around Santa Ana La Real is varied, reflecting its location within the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park. You'll hike through Mediterranean forests, cork oak and holm oak groves, and along river valleys. Paths can range from well-maintained tracks to more rugged, natural surfaces, with some elevation changes, particularly on routes leading to viewpoints or through hilly sections.
tips: Castaño del Robledo is a charming town in the province of Huelva, Andalusia, known for its stunning natural beauty and rich history, it is a perfect destination to unwind and enjoy nature and Andalusian culture.

The Hermitage of the Queen of the Angels is a Catholic temple located in the Peña de Arias Montano, belonging to the town of Alájar. Every September 8, it hosts the pilgrimage of the Queen of the Angels, the one that summons the most pilgrims in the Sierra de Aracena region.

The Camino de Santa Ana to Alájar is a popular hiking route in the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche, in the province of Huelva. This trail connects the towns of Santa Ana la Real and Alájar, offering a picturesque route through natural landscapes and points of historical interest.😊

waypoints.name: Arias Montano Peak and the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Angels
